claim: A feeding station is one of the most important tools in lost dog recovery — aromatic food placed at last-sighted location attracts the dog back to a fixed, monitorable point.
stance_target: feeding station as primary anchor point for lost dog recovery
scope_conditions: all lost dog scenarios; particularly effective for dogs that have entered flight mode
quoted_support: A feeding station is one of the most important tools in the recovery a lost dog. To set up a food station, put stinky food on the ground that will help attract your lost dog back to a location where it was sighted.

claim: Liquid smoke (hickory/mesquite) spray dramatically enhances feeding station scent range when applied to ground, tree trunks, and nearby vegetation.
stance_target: food-based scent enhancement (liquid smoke) for feeding stations
scope_conditions: outdoor feeding stations; wind-dependent scent dispersion
quoted_support: To enhance the feeding stations we use liquid smoke. It is a pungent hickory, mesquite smell... a BBQ in a bottle!! Spray it on the ground near the food and trail it. Spray the trunks of trees and nearby vegetation to help hold the scent.

claim: A feeding station without a camera monitoring who ate the food prolongs the search — identifying whether the target dog or wildlife consumed the food is critical for next-step decisions.
stance_target: camera monitoring of feeding stations as mandatory, not optional
scope_conditions: all feeding station deployments; wildlife competition (raccoons, cats, skunks, opossums) is unavoidable
quoted_support: A feeding station should ALWAYS be properly monitored with a trail camera or home surveillance camera such as Ring or Nest. Without knowing what is eating the food it can prolong your search.

claim: Once a lost dog finds a food source it will likely return — camera-confirmed feeding time-stamps provide the scheduling intelligence needed to time a trap deployment.
stance_target: feeding pattern detection as the trigger condition for trap deployment
scope_conditions: dogs that have not been picked up by humans; hungry dogs in flight mode
quoted_support: When the lost dog goes to the food station, it takes a photo and puts a time stamp on the photo, which will help you determine your missing dog's feeding pattern. Once your dog finds the food source, he/she will most likely return. Now you can move forward with the next step of capture and getting a trap set if necessary.
